narrative.context: You sent a message to a close friend about meeting after class. The message shows 'seen' but there’s no reply for hours.

options.label: Pause and read the cues
options.description: Notice your body cues and the uncertainty. Check for other signals (their recent tone, context, timing) before assuming intent.

ei.rationale: Accurately perceives emotion signals under ambiguity (your own anxiety + limited external cues) without jumping to conclusions.

options.label: Send one neutral follow-up
options.description: Send a short check-in: 'Hey—just checking if you’re free later. No rush.'

ei.rationale: Keeps interpretation tentative and seeks more data, but may still be driven by discomfort with ambiguity.

options.label: Assume they’re upset and apologize
options.description: Send: 'Sorry if I upset you—did I do something wrong?'

ei.rationale: Misreads limited cues as evidence of conflict; increases emotional load without confirming the situation.

options.label: Post a pointed story/status
options.description: Indirectly express irritation online so they notice.

ei.rationale: Amplifies ambiguity and social threat; avoids accurate perception and direct information-gathering.
